note: First artificial satellite.
Carried Laika, the first animal to orbit Earth.
First successful United States satellite; its data helped reveal the Van Allen radiation belts.
First solar-powered satellite and the oldest human-made object still orbiting Earth.
Confirmed and expanded measurements of the Van Allen radiation belts.
Large scientific satellite carrying instruments to study Earth's upper atmosphere and near-Earth space.
Measured charged particles in Earth's radiation belts and contributed to the discovery of the outer belt.
Failed to reach lunar orbit but travelled far into space and returned scientific measurements.
Failed to reach the Moon but climbed to about 1,530 kilometres and returned scientific data.
Failed to fly by the Moon but returned valuable measurements of Earth's radiation belts.
First communications satellite and the first use of an Atlas rocket to place a payload into orbit.
Missed its planned lunar impact, flew past the Moon and became the first spacecraft to enter heliocentric orbit.
Early weather-observation satellite designed to measure cloud-cover distribution.
Flew past the Moon farther away than planned and became the first United States spacecraft to enter heliocentric orbit.
First spacecraft stabilized in all three axes and first to send a reentry capsule back from orbit, but the capsule was not recovered.
Studied Earth's magnetosphere and transmitted the first photograph of Earth taken by a satellite in orbit.
Reached orbit, but a power-system failure prevented the planned recovery mission.
Reached orbit, but a retrorocket malfunction prevented recovery of the capsule.
First human-made object to reach the surface of another celestial body by impacting the Moon.
Returned the first photographs of the far side of the Moon.
Studied energetic particles, micrometeoroids and Earth's radiation balance.
Reached orbit, but spacecraft tumbling prevented the planned reconnaissance and recovery objectives.
Reached an incorrect orbit and the reentry capsule could not be recovered.
First successful weather satellite.
Early passive communications satellite.
Belka and Strelka returned safely from orbit.
First spacecraft to fly past Venus, though contact was lost before encounter.
Yuri Gagarin became the first human in space.
Alan Shepard became the first American in space.
Crewed suborbital Mercury flight with Gus Grissom.
Gherman Titov completed a day-long orbital mission.
John Glenn became the first American to orbit Earth.
Mercury orbital mission flown by Scott Carpenter.
Pioneering active communications satellite.
Joint mission flown near Vostok 4.
Joint mission flown near Vostok 3.
First successful planetary flyby, at Venus.
Mercury orbital mission flown by Wally Schirra.
Final crewed Mercury mission, flown by Gordon Cooper.
Valery Bykovsky set a solo spaceflight endurance record.
Valentina Tereshkova became the first woman in space.
First satellite placed in geosynchronous orbit.
First uncrewed Gemini test flight.
First multi-person crewed spacecraft.
First successful Mars flyby and close-up images.
Uncrewed suborbital Gemini test.
Returned thousands of close-up lunar images before impact.
Alexei Leonov performed the first spacewalk.
First crewed Gemini mission.
Ed White performed the first American spacewalk.
Eight-day endurance mission.
Fourteen-day endurance mission.
First crewed rendezvous with another spacecraft.
First successful soft landing on the Moon.
First docking of two spacecraft in orbit.
First artificial satellite of the Moon.
First successful U.S. soft landing on the Moon.
Crewed rendezvous and EVA mission.
Docking and high-altitude rendezvous mission.
First U.S. spacecraft to orbit the Moon.
Set a crewed Earth-orbit altitude record.
Final Gemini mission.
No launch occurred; the crew died during a prelaunch test.
Second successful U.S. lunar soft landing.
Vladimir Komarov died during the failed return.
First successful in-situ atmospheric measurements at another planet.
Successful Venus flyby.
Lunar surface composition measurements.
First spacecraft to launch from the lunar surface and land again.
First uncrewed test of Saturn V.
First uncrewed Lunar Module flight.
Final uncrewed Saturn V qualification flight.
First living organisms to orbit the Moon and return safely.
First crewed Apollo mission.
First humans to orbit the Moon.
First crewed flight of the complete Apollo spacecraft.
Full dress rehearsal for a lunar landing.
Sample-return attempt that crashed on the Moon.
First crewed Moon landing.
Second crewed Moon landing.
Japan's first satellite.
Lunar landing aborted; crew returned safely.
China's first satellite.
First successful transmission from the surface of another planet.
First robotic lunar sample-return mission.
Delivered the first successful robotic lunar rover.
Third crewed Moon landing.
First space station.
Orbiter succeeded; lander crashed on Mars.
First soft landing on Mars, but transmission ended quickly.
First spacecraft to orbit another planet.
First Apollo mission to use the Lunar Roving Vehicle.
First and only British satellite launched by a British rocket.
First spacecraft to traverse the asteroid belt and visit Jupiter.
Fifth crewed Moon landing.
First satellite of the Landsat Earth-observation program.
Final Apollo lunar landing mission.
First spacecraft to visit Saturn.
Launch of the United States' first space station.
First crewed Skylab mission.
Second crewed Skylab mission.
First mission to use a gravity assist and first to visit Mercury.
Final crewed Skylab mission.
Solar probe that approached closer to the Sun than any earlier spacecraft.
Returned the first images from the surface of Venus.
Successful Venus orbiter and lander.
First international crewed spaceflight partnership.
Docked with the U.S. Apollo spacecraft.
Orbiter and lander mission to Mars.
Second Viking orbiter and lander mission to Mars.
Military Almaz space station.
Final Soviet lunar sample-return mission.
Grand Tour mission to the outer planets.
Outer-planet mission now operating in interstellar space.
Long-duration space station with two docking ports.
Long-running ultraviolet space observatory.
Mapped Venus from orbit.
Sent multiple probes into the atmosphere of Venus.
First spacecraft placed at an Earth–Sun Lagrange point.
First launch of Europe's Ariane rocket family.
Solar observatory later repaired by astronauts in orbit.
First orbital flight of the Space Shuttle.
Long-lived Venus lander with color surface images.
Successful Venus orbiter and lander.
Final Salyut space station.
First operational Space Shuttle mission.
First infrared space telescope to survey most of the sky.

Radar mapping mission at Venus.

First Spacelab mission.
First untethered spacewalk using the Manned Maneuvering Unit.
Kathryn Sullivan became the first American woman to perform a spacewalk.

Studied Venus and Halley's Comet.
Japan's first interplanetary spacecraft.
Flew close to Halley's Comet.
Japanese mission to Halley's Comet.
First flight of Space Shuttle Atlantis.
Challenger and its seven-person crew were lost shortly after launch.
Launch of the core module of the Mir space station.
First flight of the Energia heavy-lift rocket; payload failed to reach orbit.
Mars and Phobos mission lost en route.
Reached Mars orbit but contact was lost before the Phobos encounter.
Only orbital flight of the uncrewed Buran spaceplane.
Radar mapping mission to Venus.
First spacecraft to orbit Jupiter.
Measured the cosmic microwave background.
Launch of the Hubble Space Telescope.
First mission to study the Sun's polar regions.
Second of NASA's Great Observatories.
ESA Earth-observation satellite.
Solar X-ray observatory.
First flight of Space Shuttle Endeavour.
Precision ocean-altimetry mission.
Contact was lost shortly before Mars orbit insertion.
Corrected Hubble's optical flaw.
Mapped the Moon and tested spacecraft technologies.
Long-running solar observatory at the Sun–Earth L1 point.
First spacecraft to orbit and land on an asteroid.
Long-duration mapping mission at Mars.
Delivered the Sojourner rover to Mars.
Explored Saturn and landed Huygens on Titan.
Mapped lunar composition and gravity.
Demonstrated ion propulsion and other advanced technologies.
First module of the International Space Station.
Connected the first U.S. ISS module to Zarya.
Contact was lost during Mars landing.
Returned samples from comet Wild 2.
Third of NASA's Great Observatories.

Second pair of Cluster II satellites.
First long-duration resident crew of the ISS.

Mapped the cosmic microwave background.
Returned solar-wind samples despite a hard landing.
Measured changes in Earth's gravity field.
Earth-observation mission studying the water cycle.
Gamma-ray space observatory.
Columbia and its seven-person crew were lost during reentry.

First spacecraft to orbit Mercury.

Solar observatory.

Mars polar lander.
Comprehensive lunar orbiter mission.
First mission to orbit two extraterrestrial bodies: Vesta and Ceres.

Gamma-ray observatory.
First privately developed liquid-fueled rocket to reach orbit.

Discovered thousands of exoplanet candidates.
Two major European space observatories launched together.
Lunar mapping and water-ice investigation missions.
Infrared all-sky survey telescope.
Continuous high-resolution observation of the Sun.
First successful interplanetary solar sail.
First flight of Falcon 9.
Second Chinese lunar mission; later visited asteroid Toutatis.
Final Space Shuttle mission.
Jupiter polar orbiter.
Twin spacecraft mapped the Moon's gravity field.
China's first prototype space station.
Delivered the Curiosity rover to Mars.

First crewed docking with Tiangong-1 and first Chinese woman in space.
Studied Earth's radiation belts.
Solar interface-region observatory.
Studied the Moon's thin exosphere and dust.
India's first interplanetary mission.
Studies the Martian upper atmosphere.

Precision three-dimensional survey of the Milky Way.
Tested lunar sample-return reentry technology.
Returned samples from asteroid Ryugu.

Space-weather observatory at Sun–Earth L1.
Demonstrated technology for gravitational-wave detection in space.

Returned samples from asteroid Bennu.
Chinese space laboratory.
Crewed mission to Tiangong-2.

Measures changes in Earth's ice sheets and vegetation.
Joint mission to Mercury.

Studies the Sun and its polar regions.
